title: Phenol-chloroform DNA purification
author: Tomasz Suchan
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.re6d3he
materials: Discard original tube. The tube contains phenol-chloroform.

steps.title: To 100ul eluted DNA, add 0
steps.instruction: To 100ul eluted DNA, add 0.5 ul of 20% SDS (*this may not be necessary as there are no cells) and 100 ul of phenol-chloroform.

steps.title: Centrifuge at room temperature for 5...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at room temperature for 5 min, at full speed (14,000 rpm).

steps.title: Pipette the aqueous phase (upper phase,...
steps.instruction: Pipette the aqueous phase (upper phase, aprox 80 ul, it is better to leave some DNA than to pipette phenol) to a new labeled tube.

steps.title: Add 1/10 volume Na acetate 3M pH 4
steps.instruction: Add 1/10 volume Na acetate 3M pH 4.8 or 5.2 (ie 8ul for 80 ul DNA solution) add 2 volumes ETOH 100% (storage -20°C) = 176 ul Total volume: 264ul, possible with 264 ng of starting DNA - otherwise, you must add a carrier: tRNA, glycogen or linear acrylamide.

steps.title: Put on dry ice for 30 min
steps.instruction: Put on dry ice for 30 min. or overnight at -20°C

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4°C for 30 min at 14,000 rpm
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4°C for 30 min at 14,000 rpm.

steps.title: Centrifuge at 4°C for 5 min at 14,000 rpm
steps.instruction: Centrifuge at 4°C for 5 min at 14,000 rpm.

steps.title: Pipette out the last drop of EtOH
steps.instruction: Pipette out the last drop of EtOH

steps.title: Speed Vac for 3' or 5-7 min at room temperature
steps.instruction: Speed Vac for 3' or 5-7 min at room temperature.

steps.title: Resuspend in 20ul of water ot...
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 20ul of water ot Tris 10 mM pH 7.5 or 8.0
